question_structure.context: An object with mass $m = 1\text{kg}$ is placed on a fixed rough inclined plane with an angle of $\theta = 37^\circ$. A force $F_1 = 10\text{N}$ is applied parallel to the incline in the upward direction, causing the object to slide up the incline at a constant velocity. Here, $\mathrm{g} = 10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$, $\sin 37^\circ = 0.6$, and $\cos 37^\circ = 0.8$.
question_structure.sub_question_1: Determine the coefficient of kinetic friction between the object and the inclined plane.
question_structure.sub_question_2: If a downward thrust parallel to the inclined plane $F_2 = 5\text{N}$ is applied to the object, determine whether the object can remain in equilibrium. If not, find the value of its acceleration $a$.

sub_question_1.step_1: Decompose the force along the inclined plane and perpendicular to the inclined plane. In the direction perpendicular to the inclined plane: $N_{1} = mg \cos \theta$
sub_question_1.step_2: Along the direction of the inclined plane: $F_{1} = mg \sin \theta + f_{1}$
sub_question_1.step_4: Solving the system of equations yields: $\mu = 0.5$

sub_question_2.step_5: The object cannot be in equilibrium. Resolve the force along the inclined plane and perpendicular to the inclined plane. Perpendicular to the inclined plane direction: $N_{2} = mg \cos \theta$
sub_question_2.step_6: Along the inclined plane direction: $F_{2} + mg \sin \theta - f_{2} = ma$

image_captions: The image shows two diagrams, labeled Fig A and Fig B, of a block resting on an inclined plane with an angle of θ. In Fig A, a force F1 is applied up the plane, parallel to its surface, while in Fig B, a force F2 is applied down the plane, at an angle to its surface. Both blocks and inclined planes are identically sized.
